evidence_quotes.quote: The practice of "Games as a Service" is inherently anti-consumer and easily abused to erase beloved games from existence for the sole purpose of eliminating them as competition for newer products by the same developer/publisher.

paragraphs.text: The practice of "Games as a Service" is inherently anti-consumer and easily abused to erase beloved games from existence for the sole purpose of eliminating them as competition for newer products by the same developer/publisher. People are derived of what in all sanity ought to be their property, for the sole purpose of maximising corporate earnings. This is not an acceptable state of things and regulation that guarantees consumers their right to products they have bought with their hard-earned money is sorely required.
